Plaintiff Pensiri Cohn, Nathan Cohn Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Jan-14-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. LAUREN CASE, ESQ. of Hayes Scott Bonino Ellingson & McLay, Tel No. 650-486-2891 appearing for Defendant Daniel O'Neill;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Defense counsel stipulates to the matter being heard by the pro tem judge. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to Plaintiffs Pensiri Cohn and Nathan Cohn's Motion For Protective Order, Plaintiffs seek an order precluding the attendance of non-party Cindy O'Neill at the deposition of Greg Burghart. However, plaintiffs have failed to establish good cause for such an order as required by CCP 2020.420. Ms. O'Neill appears to have attended a similar deposition in a similar case with an experienced Discovery Referee present, yet the Referee seems not to have admonished Ms. O'Neill in any way. Suspicions, non-deposition conduct and facial expressions do not amount to good cause for a protective order. If Ms. O'Neill or anyone else should prove to be disruptive during a deposition, the Code provides a remedy - suspend the deposition and seek a protective order. The motion is denied. Rather than exacerbate the acrimony which seems to afflict this case, sanctions are likewise denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge Pro Tem: James H. Fleming, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/JPT)
